IsA: It is a late-nineteenth-century watercolor portrait.
It is a portrait of Bartley Harris.
It is a work by Maria Howard Weeden.
HasA: It has a unique rendering style.
It has a realistic representation of an individual.
It has a broad and cosmopolitan audience.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by nearly photographic images.
It is characterized by lines so delicate they are nearly indistinguishable.
It is characterized by intricate brushwork.
It is characterized by its stark contrast to common representations of African Americans.
It is characterized by its realistic representation of an individual with dignity and humanity.
UsedFor: It is used for highlighting Weeden’s facility in creating nearly photographic images.
It is used for showcasing intricate brushwork.
It is used for contrasting common representations of African Americans.
It is used for representing an individual with dignity and humanity.
It is used for earning Weeden additional fame and commissions.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Weeden House Museum.
It is located in Huntsville, Alabama.
It is located in the Twickenham Historic District.
It is located in the Weeden House Museum in Huntsville, Alabama.
It is located in the Weeden House Museum in the Twickenham Historic District of Huntsville, Alabama.
BuiltIn: It was built in the late nineteenth century.
CreatedBy: It was created by Maria Howard Weeden.
CreatedIn: It was created in the late nineteenth century.
It was created in Huntsville, Alabama.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Maria Howard Weeden.
PartOf: It is part of the collection at the Weeden House Museum.
It is part of Maria Howard Weeden's body of work.
It is part of the history of portraiture in Huntsville, Alabama.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Weeden House Museum.
It belongs to the collection of Maria Howard Weeden's works.
KnownFor: It is known for its realistic representation of an individual.
It is known for its intricate brushwork.
It is known for being a work by Maria Howard Weeden.
It is known for its unique style contrasting with period depictions of African Americans.
It is known for its exhibition in Berlin, Germany.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Bartley Harris.
It is associated with Maria Howard Weeden.
It is associated with the Saint Bartley Primitive Baptist Church.
It is associated with the Weeden House Museum.
It is associated with late-nineteenth-century Huntsville.
NamedAfter: It is named after Bartley Harris.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the late nineteenth century.
It occurred in Berlin, Germany in 1896.
HeldIn: It was held in Berlin, Germany in 1896.
It was held in a gallery in Berlin, Germany.
About: It is about a prominent and influential public figure in late-nineteenth-century Huntsville.
It is about the life and legacy of Bartley Harris.
It is about the artistic style and techniques of Maria Howard Weeden.
It is about the representation of African Americans in late-nineteenth-century art.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Maria Howard Weeden's artistic training.
It was influenced by William Frye's portraiture techniques.
It was influenced by Weeden's experimentation in method and technique.
It was influenced by the World's Columbian Exhibition in Chicago.
It was influenced by Weeden's dedication to fair and realistic artistic representation.
Influenced: It influenced Weeden's fame and commissions.
It influenced the perception of African Americans in art.
It influenced the historical understanding of Bartley Harris.
It influenced the Saint Bartley Primitive Baptist Church community.
